title: Ixora ferrea
taxonomy: Ixora ferrea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Ixora, which consists of around 400 species of flowering plants. The genus Ixora is further classified into 12 to 18 sections, depending on the source. Ixora ferrea is a species that is native to tropical regions. It is often used as an ornamental plant due to its attractive flowers and compact growth habit.
uses: Ixora ferrea, commonly known as the jungle geranium or red-eyed geranium, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential candidate for the treatment of several health conditions. In Ayurvedic medicine, Ixora ferrea is used to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and flowers are also used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential applications of Ixora ferrea.
